exercises.title: What triggers this workflow?
exercises.instructions:
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main, 'release/*']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1'
```
List all trigger scenarios.
exercises.expected_solution:
1. git push to 'main' branch → triggers
2. git push to any 'release/*' branch (release/v1.0, release/v2.1) → triggers
3. Pull request opened/updated targeting 'main' → triggers
4. Scheduled: every Monday at 2:00 AM UTC → triggers
   (cron format: minute=0, hour=2, day=*, month=*, weekday=1)

PR flow: open PR → workflow runs (CI) → merge → push triggers again (deploy)
Scheduled: weekly security scan, dependency audit, or integration test suite

test_cases.expected:
1. git push to 'main' branch → triggers
2. git push to any 'release/*' branch (release/v1.0, release/v2.1) → triggers
3. Pull request opened/updated targeting 'main' → triggers
4. Scheduled: every Monday at 2:00 AM UTC → triggers
   (cron format: minute=0, hour=2, day=*, month=*, weekday=1)

PR flow: open PR → workflow runs (CI) → merge → push triggers again (deploy)
Scheduled: weekly security scan, dependency audit, or integration test suite

hints: cron: '0 2 * * 1' = minute 0, hour 2 UTC, every Monday
push and pull_request are the most common triggers
schedule: useful for nightly builds or weekly dependency audits

exercises.title: Complete the GitHub Actions job
exercises.instructions: Fill in cache strategy and test command.
exercises.starter_code:
- name: Set up Java 21
  uses: actions/setup-java@v4
  with:
    java-version: '21'
    distribution: '___'
    cache: ___
- name: Run tests
  run: mvn test --___-mode
- name: Upload test results
  if: ___
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
exercises.expected_solution:
- name: Set up Java 21
  uses: actions/setup-java@v4
  with:
    java-version: '21'
    distribution: 'temurin'
    cache: maven
- name: Run tests
  run: mvn test --batch-mode
- name: Upload test results
  if: always()
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4

blanks.hint: uploads even on test failure — you want failure reports!

hints: temurin: free OpenJDK distribution (Eclipse Adoptium)
cache: maven: skips re-downloading Maven dependencies
always(): conditional that runs even if previous steps failed

exercises.title: Write a basic test-only CI workflow
exercises.instructions:
Write a GitHub Actions workflow:
1. Triggers on push + PR to main
2. Java 21 with Maven cache
3. mvn test
4. Upload test results even on failure
5. Fail fast: fail the workflow if tests fail
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI — Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Run Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: Checkout
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: '21'
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven   # caches ~/.m2 — saves 1-2 min per run
    
    - name: Run tests
      run: mvn test --batch-mode
      # --batch-mode: no interactive prompts (required for CI)
      # fails the step (exit code 1) if any test fails
      # GitHub Actions: step failure → job failure → workflow failure
    
    - name: Upload test reports
      if: always()    # run even if tests fail!
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: surefire-reports
        path: target/surefire-reports/
        retention-days: 7
hints: always(): uploads test XML even when they fail
retention-days: controls how long artifacts are stored (default 90 days)
--batch-mode: prevents CI from hanging on Maven prompts

exercises.title: Add Docker build and push to workflow
exercises.instructions:
Extend the CI workflow to:
1. Build Docker image (only on push to main)
2. Login to GHCR (GitHub Container Registry)
3. Push with SHA tag
4. Cache Docker layers via GitHub Actions cache
exercises.expected_solution:
build-push:
    name: Build and Push Docker Image
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write   # required to push to GHCR
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Login to GitHub Container Registry
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        registry: ghcr.io
        username: ${{ github.actor }}
        password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        # GITHUB_TOKEN: auto-provided by GitHub Actions, no setup needed
    
    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      # Buildx: enables layer caching and multi-platform builds
    
    - name: Build and Push
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        push: true
        tags: |
          ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }}
          ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:latest
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
        # cache: reuses Docker layers from previous runs
        # mode=max: caches all layers, not just the final image
hints: GITHUB_TOKEN: auto-provided, no secret setup needed for GHCR
github.sha: immutable commit-level tag
packages: write permission: required to push to ghcr.io

exercises.title: Add Kubernetes deployment to CI/CD
exercises.instructions:
Add a deploy job:
1. depends on build-push
2. Uses KUBECONFIG_STAGING secret
3. kubectl set image with SHA tag
4. rollout status with timeout
5. GitHub Environment: staging (with protection)
exercises.expected_solution:
deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    needs: build-push
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: staging           # GitHub Environment with protection rules
      url: https://staging.razorpay.internal
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Configure kubectl
      run: |
        mkdir -p ~/.kube
        echo '${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_STAGING }}' > ~/.kube/config
        chmod 600 ~/.kube/config
    
    - name: Deploy new image
      run: |
        kubectl set image deployment/payment-service \
          payment-service=ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }} \
          --namespace payments
        # OR: update image in YAML and kubectl apply:
        # sed -i 's/payment-service:.*/payment-service:${{ github.sha }}/' k8s/deployment.yml
        # kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yml
    
    - name: Wait for rollout
      run: |
        kubectl rollout status deployment/payment-service \
          --namespace payments \
          --timeout=5m
        # Fails if deployment doesn't complete in 5 minutes
        # GitHub Actions step failure → workflow failure
    
    - name: Verify health
      run: |
        POD=$(kubectl get pods -n payments -l app=payment-service \
          -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
        kubectl exec $POD -n payments -- \
          wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/actuator/health | grep '"status":"UP"'
hints: environment: staging: creates protection gate in GitHub UI
KUBECONFIG_STAGING: stored as Actions secret — never in code
rollout status exit code: 0=success, 1=timeout/failure → fails the step

exercises.title: Matrix builds for multiple Java versions
exercises.instructions:
Write a matrix strategy testing on Java 17 and 21:
1. matrix.java-version: [17, 21]
2. Setup Java with matrix value
3. Run tests on both versions
4. Explain: when matrix testing Java versions matters
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/matrix-test.yml
name: Matrix Tests

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test-matrix:
    name: Test on Java ${{ matrix.java-version }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        java-version: [17, 21]
      fail-fast: false  # run all matrix jobs even if one fails
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Set up Java ${{ matrix.java-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ matrix.java-version }}
        distribution: temurin
        cache: maven
    - name: Run tests
      run: mvn test --batch-mode

# WHEN MATRIX JAVA TESTING MATTERS:
# 1. Library/SDK authoring: support multiple Java LTS versions (17 + 21)
# 2. Migration testing: moving from 17 to 21 — tests both during transition
# 3. New Java feature adoption: test new features work in 21, still compile in 17
# 4. Open source projects: users may run on different Java versions
#
# For most microservices (internal): single Java version is fine.
# Matrix overhead: 2× test time. Worth it for libraries, rarely needed for services.
hints: matrix.java-version: creates one job per entry
fail-fast: false: see ALL failures, not just the first
${{ matrix.java-version }}: reference matrix value in steps

exercises.title: GitHub Actions secrets management
exercises.instructions:
Document how to safely manage secrets in GitHub Actions:
1. Store DB_PASSWORD as GitHub repository secret
2. Reference in workflow
3. What should NEVER go in workflow YAML
4. Organization-level vs environment-level secrets
5. How to rotate secrets without downtime
exercises.expected_solution:
# GITHUB ACTIONS SECRETS MANAGEMENT

# 1. Create repository secret:
# GitHub UI: Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret
# Name: DB_PASSWORD_STAGING
# Value: (actual password)
# Or: GitHub CLI: gh secret set DB_PASSWORD_STAGING

# 2. Reference in workflow (masked in logs):
workflow_reference = '''
    env:
      DB_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD_STAGING }}
    # OR as build arg:
      run: docker build --build-arg DB_PASS=${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD_STAGING }} .
    # OR in k8s secret creation:
      run: |
        kubectl create secret generic payment-secrets \
          --from-literal=db_password=${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD_STAGING }}
'''

# 3. NEVER put in workflow YAML:
never_in_yaml = '''
    # BAD:
    env:
      DB_PASSWORD: payments123    ← hardcoded secret in code!
      API_KEY: rzp_live_abc123    ← visible in git history forever!
    # GitHub will scan for hardcoded secrets and alert you, but prevention > cure
'''

# 4. Secret scopes:
scopes = '''
    Repository secret: available to all workflows in the repo
    Environment secret: only available when deploying to that environment
      (use for KUBECONFIG_PROD — only accessible when deploying to production)
    Organization secret: shared across repos (useful for shared ECR credentials)
'''

# 5. Rotate without downtime:
rotation = '''
    Step 1: Create new credential in external system (e.g., new DB password)
    Step 2: Update GitHub secret to new value
    Step 3: kubectl rollout restart deployment/payment-service
            (picks up new secret from GitHub Actions on next deploy)
    Zero downtime: old pods continue running with old secret
                   new pods (rolling update) pick up new secret
'''

public class SecretsGuide { public static void main(String[] a) { System.out.println("Secrets: stored in GitHub, never in YAML"); } }

expected_stdout_contains: Secrets: stored in GitHub, never in YAML

hints: GitHub masks secret values in logs automatically
Environment secrets: more granular than repo secrets
Git history: once a secret is committed, it's compromised — rotate immediately

exercises.title: Complete CI/CD workflow with staging + prod gates
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete pipeline:
1. test job (all pushes)
2. build-push job (push to main only)
3. deploy-staging (automatic after build)
4. deploy-prod (requires manual approval via GitHub Environment)
5. Rollback job: runs only if deploy-prod fails
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/full-cicd.yml
name: Payment Service Full CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with: { java-version: '21', distribution: temurin, cache: maven }
    - run: mvn test --batch-mode
    - if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with: { name: test-results, path: target/surefire-reports/ }

  build-push:
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions: { contents: read, packages: write }
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with: { registry: ghcr.io, username: '${{ github.actor }}', password: '${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}' }
    - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
    - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        push: true
        tags: ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build-push
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
    - run: |
        echo '${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_STAGING }}' > /tmp/kube.conf
        kubectl set image deployment/payment-service payment-service=ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }} -n payments
        kubectl rollout status deployment/payment-service -n payments --timeout=5m
      env: { KUBECONFIG: /tmp/kube.conf }

  deploy-prod:
    needs: deploy-staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: production        # requires manual approval in GitHub UI
    steps:
    - run: |
        echo '${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_PROD }}' > /tmp/kube.conf
        kubectl set image deployment/payment-service payment-service=ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }} -n payments
        kubectl rollout status deployment/payment-service -n payments --timeout=10m
      env: { KUBECONFIG: /tmp/kube.conf }

  rollback-prod:
    needs: deploy-prod
    if: failure()    # only if deploy-prod fails
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - run: |
        echo '${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_PROD }}' > /tmp/kube.conf
        kubectl rollout undo deployment/payment-service -n payments
        kubectl rollout status deployment/payment-service -n payments --timeout=5m
      env: { KUBECONFIG: /tmp/kube.conf }
hints: environment: production: requires manual approval (configure in GitHub Settings)
if: failure(): runs only when the referenced job fails
Automatic staging + manual prod gate: most common production deployment pattern

exercises.title: Integration tests with service containers
exercises.instructions:
Run integration tests in GitHub Actions with a real Postgres container:
1. services block for postgres:15
2. Wait for postgres to be ready
3. Pass JDBC URL to test
4. Show: how this mirrors the local docker-compose setup
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/integration-test.yml
jobs:
  integration-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: payments_test
          POSTGRES_USER: test_user
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: testpass
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
      redis:
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports: ['6379:6379']
        options: --health-cmd 'redis-cli ping' --health-interval 10s --health-retries 5
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with: { java-version: '21', distribution: temurin, cache: maven }
    - name: Run integration tests
      run: mvn verify --batch-mode -Pintegration-test
      env:
        SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/payments_test
        SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME: test_user
        SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD: testpass
        SPRING_DATA_REDIS_HOST: localhost
        SPRING_DATA_REDIS_PORT: '6379'
    # options: health checks (same as docker-compose healthcheck)
    # GitHub Actions waits for health check before starting steps
hints: services block: GitHub Actions starts these containers before job steps
options: --health-cmd: same as docker-compose healthcheck
ports: required to connect from step commands (localhost:5432)

exercises.title: Workflow job execution order
exercises.instructions:
```yaml
jobs:
  unit-test:
    ...
  integration-test:
    ...
  build:
    needs: [unit-test, integration-test]
  deploy-staging:
    needs: build
  deploy-prod:
    needs: deploy-staging
    environment: production
  notify:
    needs: [deploy-staging, deploy-prod]
    if: always()
```

1. Which jobs run in parallel?
2. When does deploy-prod run?
3. When does notify run?
4. If integration-test fails, which jobs are skipped?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. PARALLEL jobs:
   unit-test and integration-test run in parallel (no 'needs' between them).
   They both run immediately when the workflow starts.

2. deploy-prod runs:
   After deploy-staging completes successfully AND
   After a human approves the 'production' environment deployment.
   The manual approval gate can delay deploy-prod indefinitely.

3. notify runs:
   After BOTH deploy-staging AND deploy-prod finish.
   'if: always()' means it runs regardless of their success/failure.
   Use case: send Slack notification on success or failure.

4. If integration-test fails:
   build: SKIPPED (needs both tests to pass)
   deploy-staging: SKIPPED (depends on build)
   deploy-prod: SKIPPED (depends on deploy-staging)
   notify: RUNS (if: always() — still notifies about the failure)
   unit-test: continues normally (parallel, not dependent on integration-test)

test_cases.expected:
1. PARALLEL jobs:
   unit-test and integration-test run in parallel (no 'needs' between them).
   They both run immediately when the workflow starts.

2. deploy-prod runs:
   After deploy-staging completes successfully AND
   After a human approves the 'production' environment deployment.
   The manual approval gate can delay deploy-prod indefinitely.

3. notify runs:
   After BOTH deploy-staging AND deploy-prod finish.
   'if: always()' means it runs regardless of their success/failure.
   Use case: send Slack notification on success or failure.

4. If integration-test fails:
   build: SKIPPED (needs both tests to pass)
   deploy-staging: SKIPPED (depends on build)
   deploy-prod: SKIPPED (depends on deploy-staging)
   notify: RUNS (if: always() — still notifies about the failure)
   unit-test: continues normally (parallel, not dependent on integration-test)

hints: No needs = parallel
needs: [a, b] = wait for both a AND b
if: always() = run even if dependencies failed

exercises.title: Workflow for automatic semantic versioning
exercises.instructions:
Write a workflow that:
1. On push to main: reads version from pom.xml
2. Creates a git tag (v1.2.3)
3. Builds Docker image with version tag
4. Creates GitHub Release with changelog
5. Show: how to auto-increment version from commit messages
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/release.yml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ['src/**', 'pom.xml']  # only when code changes

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions: { contents: write, packages: write }
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with: { fetch-depth: 0 }  # full history for changelog
    
    - name: Get version from pom.xml
      id: version
      run: |
        VERSION=$(mvn help:evaluate -Dexpression=project.version -q -DforceStdout)
        echo "version=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    
    - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with: { java-version: '21', distribution: temurin, cache: maven }
    
    - name: Build + test
      run: mvn clean package --batch-mode -DskipTests=false
    
    - name: Create git tag
      run: |
        git config user.name 'github-actions[bot]'
        git config user.email 'github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com'
        git tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        git push origin v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    
    - name: Build and push Docker
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        push: true
        tags: |
          ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
          ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:latest
    
    - name: Create GitHub Release
      uses: actions/create-release@v1
      env: { GITHUB_TOKEN: '${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}' }
      with:
        tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        release_name: Payment Service v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        body: 'Auto-generated release. See commits for details.'

# FOR AUTO-INCREMENT:
# Conventional Commits: 'feat: add Visa tokenization' → minor version bump
# 'fix: null pointer in gateway' → patch bump
# 'feat!: breaking API change' → major bump
# Tools: semantic-release action handles this automatically
hints: GITHUB_OUTPUT: write step outputs (key=value)
fetch-depth: 0: full git history for changelog generation
semantic-release: automates version bumping from conventional commits

exercises.title: Security scanning in CI pipeline
exercises.instructions:
Add security scanning to the CI pipeline:
1. OWASP dependency check (Maven plugin)
2. Trivy Docker image scan
3. Fail the build if CRITICAL vulnerabilities found
4. Upload SBOM (Software Bill of Materials)
5. Show: where in the pipeline each scan fits
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/security.yml
jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with: { java-version: '21', distribution: temurin, cache: maven }
    
    # 1. OWASP dependency check (known CVEs in dependencies):
    - name: OWASP Dependency Check
      run: |
        mvn dependency-check:check \
          -DfailBuildOnCVSS=7        # fail on HIGH/CRITICAL (CVSS ≥ 7)
      continue-on-error: true      # don't fail overall, just report
    - name: Upload OWASP report
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: owasp-report
        path: target/dependency-check-report.html

    # 2. Build image first (for Trivy scan):
    - name: Build Docker image for scanning
      run: docker build -t payment-service:scan .
    
    # 3. Trivy Docker image scan:
    - name: Trivy Image Scan
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
      with:
        image-ref: 'payment-service:scan'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
        severity: 'CRITICAL'        # only CRITICAL
        exit-code: '1'              # fail if CRITICAL found
    
    # 4. Upload SARIF to GitHub Security tab:
    - name: Upload Trivy results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: trivy-results.sarif
    
    # 5. Generate SBOM:
    - name: Generate SBOM
      uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
      with:
        image: payment-service:scan
        format: spdx-json
        output-file: sbom.spdx.json
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with: { name: sbom, path: sbom.spdx.json }
hints: OWASP DC: checks your Java dependencies against CVE database
Trivy: scans Docker image for OS-level vulnerabilities
SARIF: standard security results format, shown in GitHub Security tab

exercises.title: Notify Slack on deployment success/failure
exercises.instructions:
Add Slack notifications:
1. Notify on successful deploy to prod
2. Notify on deploy failure with rollback
3. Include: commit SHA, author, environment, link to workflow run
4. Use GitHub Actions built-in expressions
hints: github.sha: short commit hash for message
github.actor: GitHub username of who triggered the workflow
github.server_url/repository/actions/runs/run_id: direct link to the workflow run

exercises.title: Reusable GitHub Actions workflow
exercises.instructions:
Create a reusable workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml with workflow_call trigger) that other repos can call:
1. Inputs: image, environment, namespace
2. Secrets: inherited from calling workflow
3. Deploy + verify steps
4. Show: how a calling workflow invokes it
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/deploy-reusable.yml
name: Reusable Deploy

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      image:
        description: Full image name with tag
        type: string
        required: true
      environment:
        description: Deployment environment
        type: string
        required: true
      namespace:
        description: Kubernetes namespace
        type: string
        default: default
    secrets:
      kubeconfig:
        description: Base64 kubeconfig for target cluster
        required: true

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    steps:
    - name: Configure kubectl
      run: |
        mkdir -p ~/.kube
        echo '${{ secrets.kubeconfig }}' | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
        chmod 600 ~/.kube/config
    - name: Deploy
      run: |
        kubectl set image deployment/payment-service \
          payment-service=${{ inputs.image }} \
          --namespace ${{ inputs.namespace }}
        kubectl rollout status deployment/payment-service \
          --namespace ${{ inputs.namespace }} --timeout=10m

# CALLING WORKFLOW:
# jobs:
#   deploy-staging:
#     uses: ./.github/workflows/deploy-reusable.yml
#     with:
#       image: ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }}
#       environment: staging
#       namespace: payments
#     secrets:
#       kubeconfig: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_STAGING }}
#
# BENEFIT: DRY — 10 services reuse the same deploy workflow
# Change deploy logic in one place → all services pick it up
hints: workflow_call: enables reuse by other workflows in same/other repos
inputs: type-checked parameters from calling workflow
base64 -d: decode kubeconfig (stored as base64 for multi-line secret)

exercises.title: Complete CI/CD for Razorpay — M21 T4 synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Write the complete Razorpay CI/CD pipeline:
1. On PR: test + integration tests with postgres service
2. On main push: test → build-push (GHCR) → deploy-staging → [manual approval] → deploy-prod
3. Rollback on prod failure
4. Slack notification on prod deploy success/failure
5. @SpringBootTest: 2 tests verifying the app works in CI context (health + counter)

All 2 tests pass.
hints: These tests run in the 'test' job in GitHub Actions
WebTestClient: tests the running Spring Boot app (not mocked)
Metrics test: validates entire Micrometer stack works from unit test
